perturbation.original_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified
perturbation.changed_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee may, at its sole discretion, include as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified
perturbation.explanation: Changing "will use commercially reasonable efforts" to "may, at its sole discretion" alters the obligation of Netzee to comply with regulatory requirements. This introduces uncertainty and shifts the responsibility, making it less enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 12 U.S. Code § 1818 - Termination of status as insured depository institution; cease-and-desist proceedings; suspension or removal of officer or director; capital directives
perturbation.law_explanation: This section of the US Code outlines the regulatory authority over banks, including the power to enforce compliance with applicable laws and regulations. By making compliance discretionary rather than obligatory, the modified text weakens the enforceability of regulatory requirements and contradicts the bank's obligation to adhere to these regulations under federal law.

Termination of status as insured depository institution (a) Termination of insurance (1) Voluntary termination Any insured depository institution which is not- (A) a national member bank; (B) a State member bank; (C) a Federal branch; (D) a Federal savings association; or (E) an insured branch which is required to be insured under subsection (a) o
perturbation.justification: The change from "will use commercially reasonable efforts" to "may, at its sole discretion" significantly weakens Netzee's obligation to comply with industry and regulatory requirements. The original text creates a reasonable expectation of compliance, whereas the changed text makes compliance optional. Snippet 1, 12 USC 1818, discusses the termination of insured depository institution status, implying the importance of adhering to regulations to maintain that status. By making compliance discretionary, the modified contract introduces a legal contradiction by undermining the enforceability of regulatory adherence, as adherence becomes uncertain, impacting the bank's obligation to adhere to regulations.

perturbation.original_text: (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.changed_text: (4) availability of basic Services with a *target* uptime between specified hours, subject to outages for any reason Netzee deems necessary.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the 'at least 99% uptime' guarantee to a 'target uptime' and adding that outages can occur for 'any reason Netzee deems necessary' significantly reduces the reliability commitment and introduces ambiguity. This is contrary to standard service level agreements (SLAs) and could be considered misleading.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code - Section 2-204: Formation in General
perturbation.law_explanation: The UCC addresses contract formation, including the necessity of a definite agreement. By changing the uptime guarantee to a mere target subject to discretionary outages, the contract becomes vague and indefinite regarding a key performance aspect, potentially rendering it unenforceable due to lack of clear agreement on service levels.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-204. Formation in General. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-204. Formation in General. (1) A contract for sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement , including conduct by both parties which recognizes the existence of such a contract . (2) An agreement sufficient to constitute a contract for sale may be found even though the moment of its making is undetermined. (3) Even though one or more terms are left open a contract for sale does not fail for indefiniteness if the parties have intended to make a contract and there is a reasonably certain basis for giving an appropriate remedy. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes a guaranteed 'at least 99% uptime' to a 'target uptime,' and adds that outages can occur for 'any reason Netzee deems necessary.' This creates a legal contradiction by using misaligned terminology which deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable. Scraped Law Snippet 1, § 2-204. Formation in General. (1) states that a contract for sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement. The change introduces ambiguity regarding service levels, contradicting the requirement for a definite agreement because the uptime guarantee is now subjective and discretionary, making it difficult to determine if there is a clear agreement on service levels.

perturbation.original_text: THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT IS GOVERNED BY, AND SHALL BE SUBJECT TO, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK, INCLUDING THE LIMITED WARRANTY AND DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY PROVIDED THEREIN.
perturbation.changed_text: THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT IS SUBJECT TO PREVAILING INDUSTRY PRACTICES, WHICH MAY SUPERSEDE THE TERMS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK.
perturbation.explanation: This clause originally clearly states the Maintenance Agreement is governed by the Master Agreement. By changing this to 'prevailing industry practices', it introduces a conflict as industry practices are undefined and potentially contradict the explicit terms of the Master Agreement, creating legal ambiguity and contradicting the express agreement of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_law: UCC § 1-303. Course of Performance, Course of Dealing, and Usage of Trade.
perturbation.law_explanation: While the UCC allows for consideration of course of performance, dealing, and trade usage, it also specifies that express terms generally control over these aspects. The modified language suggests industry practices *supersede* the Master Agreement, directly contradicting the UCC's principle that express contractual terms have priority. This undermines the enforceability of the original contract's terms.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-303. Course of Performance, Course of Dealing, and Usage of Trade. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-303. Course of Performance, Course of Dealing, and Usage of Trade. (a) A "course of performance" is a sequence of conduct between the parties to a particular transaction that exists if: (1) the agreement of the parties with respect to the transaction involves repeated occasions for performance by a party ; and (2) the other party, with knowledge of the nature of the performance and opportunity for objection to it, accepts the performance or acquiesces in it without objection. (b) A "course of dealing" is a sequence of conduct concerning previous transactions between the parties to a particular transaction that is fairly to be regarded as establishing a common basis of understanding for interpreting their expressions and other conduct. (c) A "usage of trade" is any practice or method of dealing having such regularity of observance in a place, vocation, or trade as to justify an expectation that it will be observed with respect to the transaction in question. The existence and scope of such a usage must be proved as facts. If it is established that such a usage is embodied in a trade code or similar record , the interpretation of the record is a question of law. (d) A course of performance or course of dealing between the parties or usage of trade in the vocation or trade in which they are engaged or of which they are or should be aware is relevant in ascertaining the meaning of the parties' agreement , may give particular meaning to specific terms of the agreement, and may supplement or qualify the terms of the agreement. A usage of trade applicable in the place in which part of the performance under the agreement is to occur may be so utilized as to that part of the performance. (e) Except as otherwise provided in subsection (f), the express terms of an agreement and any applicable
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a legal contradiction. The original text explicitly states the Maintenance Agreement is governed by the Master Agreement. The changed text allows 'prevailing industry practices' to supersede the Master Agreement. Scraped Law Snippet 1, specifically section (e), states that 'the express terms of an agreement' take precedence. By allowing industry practices to supersede the express terms of the Master Agreement, the changed text directly contradicts this principle, potentially undermining the enforceability of the original contract terms. The Perturbation Explanation highlights the conflict created by allowing undefined industry practices to contradict the explicit terms of the Master Agreement.
